title: Validate T-Echo PPS samples for CCP
description: Physically validate an existing, reworked, or manufacturer-supplied T-Echo with L76K 1PPS connected to the nRF52840 for LICHEN CCP scheduled mode. This is engineering validation, not production approval.
design: Capture PPS with nRF52840 GPIOTE plus PPI into a free-running hardware timer; use UART only to associate the edge with its GPS second. Measure fixed offset, jitter, polarity, pulse width, lock acquisition, warm/cold start, power, sleep/wake, oscillator holdover, antenna/environment sensitivity, and fallback behavior. Compare measured uncertainty and slot guards with the canonical simulator model and quantify capacity rather than claiming zero collisions.
acceptance_criteria: Exact sample/revision and wiring are recorded; oscilloscope or logic-analyzer evidence characterizes PPS electrical behavior; firmware captures PPS without ISR timestamp dependence and associates unambiguous GPS seconds; repeated cold/warm starts and at least indoor/outdoor or degraded-signal transitions are tested; holdover uncertainty and CH0 fallback occur at calculated boundaries; two or more radios demonstrate scheduled SF10/BW125 cells and channel switching; measured guard, collision loss, PDR, power, and capacity are compared with baseline and simulator predictions; display, LoRa, GNSS position, USB/BLE, button, RTC, and low-power regressions are checked; result recommends production pursuit, revision changes, or rejection.
